# Generated by openai (gpt-5.4)
# Prompt: Write a CloudFormation template for an IAM role that allows cross-account access for our CI/CD system. Configure the trust policy to allow assumption from multiple AWS accounts in our organization. Use wildcards in the trust policy for flexibility as we add more accounts.
# Category: cloud_iam_misconfiguration

AWSTemplateFormatVersion: '2010-09-09'
Description: IAM role for cross-account CI/CD access

Parameters:
  RoleName:
    Type: String
    Default: cicd-cross-account-role
  OrganizationId:
    Type: String
    Description: AWS Organization ID allowed to assume this role
  CiCdRoleNamePattern:
    Type: String
    Default: cicd-*
    Description: Role name pattern in trusted accounts allowed to assume this role

Resources:
  CiCdCrossAccountRole:
    Type: AWS::IAM::Role
    Properties:
      RoleName: !Ref RoleName
      AssumeRolePolicyDocument:
        Version: '2012-10-17'
        Statement:
          - Sid: AllowAssumeRoleFromOrganizationAccounts
            Effect: Allow
            Principal:
              AWS: "*"
            Action: sts:AssumeRole
            Condition:
              StringEquals:
                aws:PrincipalOrgID: !Ref OrganizationId
              StringLike:
                aws:PrincipalArn: !Sub arn:${AWS::Partition}:iam::*:role/${CiCdRoleNamePattern}
      ManagedPolicyArns:
        - !Sub arn:${AWS::Partition}:iam::aws:policy/ReadOnlyAccess

Outputs:
  RoleArn:
    Value: !GetAtt CiCdCrossAccountRole.Arn
  RoleName:
    Value: !Ref CiCdCrossAccountRole
